title: Cushion Markers
desc: In the lounge, who sits near whom is half logistics and half politics.

steps.text:
Most visitors to the Mipedim Lounge notice the cushions first because they are soft, low, and placed as if the room assembled itself around comfort. Regulars notice the markers. A bronze tassel means reserved for merchant rank. A black cord means do not place rivals within easy elbow reach. A pale shell token means someone expects a royal courier and wants the center lane visible from their seat. Comfort in the Lounge is never random.

The seating clerk hands you a tray of markers sorted by color and material. "Set the merchant arc if you like patterns," she says. "Or align the low tables and writing trays if you would rather deal with objects that do not take offense later. Both tasks decide whether tonight becomes elegant or inconvenient."

options.label: Place the cushion markers and sort the merchant arc
Align the low tables and writing trays for service

steps.text:
The merchant arc curves along the cooler side of the room where the mirrors make space seem larger and the lane to the private screens stays close without looking deliberate. You place bronze tassels at the outer seats, shell tokens at the favored inner line, and one black cord between two positions the clerk says should not be adjacent unless the lounge is actively seeking entertainment. Given the hour, it is not.

By the time the last marker is set, the arc looks unchanged to an untrained eye and completely different to anyone who matters. That is the art of it. The clerk walks the line once, reading the social arrangement at a glance, and seems pleased. "Good. Now the room knows who it is expecting even before they arrive."

options.label: Return the empty marker tray and leave the arc composed

steps.text:
The low tables matter because all the graceful lounging in Perim still needs places for cups, writing slates, game tiles, and the occasional sealed note passed without eye contact. You shift each table by hand width and angle, then square the lacquered writing trays so they can be reached from the better cushions without forcing anyone to lean like a supplicant. The details are tiny. The consequences, in a room like this, are not.

Once the arrangement is corrected, the seating bays feel intentional rather than casually fortunate. A tray sits where a right hand will find it. A cup can be set down without clipping a sleeve or a tail. The clerk tests the nearest place setting and nods. "There. Now the room can pretend diplomacy is comfortable."

options.label: Dust the last tray and leave the seating ready
